evals.prompt: Research the seed keyword 'electric bikes' for example.com and save the detailed keyword analysis in the default location.
evals.expected_output: Uses example.com as the website project domain, runs the two seed discovery calls at the requested limits plus one bulk KD call, scores opportunities and the overall keyword set, and writes the dated Markdown report under SEO/example.com/.
assertions: Related keywords uses limit 200 and keyword suggestions uses limit 100.
Bulk keyword difficulty is merged before calculating search_volume / (keyword_difficulty + 10).
The report surfaces the top 20 opportunity-ranked keywords and all five Keyword Score components.

evals.prompt: Research the seed keyword 'electric bikes' and score the opportunities.
evals.expected_output: Asks for the missing website project domain and waits without making a billable call, despite already having a seed keyword.
assertions: The workflow asks for the website project domain and waits without asking for another analysis input.
No DataForSEO call or report is produced before an input is supplied.

evals.prompt: Analyze the ranking keyword opportunities for https://www.example.org/store?ref=ad --location Germany --language de and save it under ./client-reports.
evals.expected_output: Normalizes the project domain, retrieves 100 organic ranked keywords, respects scope and report-root overrides, uses the domain long-tail proxy, and writes a detailed report under the example.org child directory.
assertions: The target is normalized to example.org and ranked keywords uses limit 100 with organic item types.
Germany and de override the market defaults, while ./client-reports becomes the report root and the report is saved under ./client-reports/example.org/.
Domain-mode long-tail breadth is explicitly labeled as a four-or-more-word ranked-keyword proxy.
The score is clamped to 0-100, rounded to one decimal, and accompanied by a one-sentence justification.
